narrative.challenge: The platform lacked a configurable analysis layer. Account Managers couldn't combine ratings, response tables, score summaries, and contextual labels into a single shared surface — creating a critical churn risk at the enterprise tier against competitors who had already moved to configurable dashboards.

projects.title: Designing Decision Clarity for a B2B Experience Management Platform
projects.description: When Account Managers can't build dashboards confidently, enterprise clients can't act on their data. I designed a real-time, modular custom dashboard that closed four critical competitive gaps and replaced a manual email reporting chain with governed live sharing.
projects.ndaNote: Employer and product details anonymised at their request. Full context available under mutual NDA.

projects.title: Designing a Unified Filter Architecture Across a Multi-Product B2B Platform
projects.description: When five products each manage filters differently, power users pay a cognitive tax every time they switch contexts. I redesigned the cross-product filter architecture from the platform up — not from the screen in.
projects.ndaNote: Employer and sensitive performance metrics anonymised at their request. Full context available under mutual NDA.

narrative.challenge: The Customer Directory was built for credit unions, healthcare networks, and K–12 institutions — letting these organisations manage contact lists, build segments, and trigger targeted surveys. I owned all design decisions end-to-end: information architecture, interaction design, usability research, stakeholder alignment, and a 47-component design system.
narrative.solution: The project's defining challenge wasn't UI complexity. It was identifying who we were actually designing for — before we shipped the wrong answer at scale.

projects.title: Scrapping $180K of engineering work — and shipping the product that was actually needed
projects.description: Six weeks into development, I discovered we were building for the wrong users. Evidence convinced leadership to pivot. The redesigned product shipped within three months.

narrative.challenge: Experience Navigator is an AI-powered strategy generation tool built for mid-to-senior CX and EX leaders who are accountable for business outcomes but lack the time and specialist depth to build cross-functional programmes from scratch.
narrative.solution: The core problem was what I framed as the Strategic Execution Gap — organisations routinely set high-level goals like "reduce churn" or "improve onboarding" but have no systematic way to convert those intentions into operational plans, survey logic, and measurable KPIs. I led UX across a 3-person team (1 designer, 1 PM, 1 developer) over a 3-month sprint, designing the end-to-end experience from goal input to a fully generated, section-by-section strategy output.

projects.title: Eliminating the Strategic Execution
projects.description: Designing an AI-powered experience strategy builder that converts a business goal into a fully operational CX/EX programme in 2 minutes.
